name: Amazon Price-Drop Alerts with Scavio + Google Sheets

parameters.content:
## Amazon Price-Drop Alerts with Scavio + Google Sheets

### How it works
This workflow tracks any number of Amazon products from a Google Sheet and sends a Gmail alert the moment the price hits or drops below your target. It is **edge-triggered** — one email per drop, no spam at flat prices — by writing the alerted price back to the sheet after every notification and only re-alerting if the price drops further.

Powered by [Scavio](https://scavio.dev), a real-time search API for Google, Amazon, Walmart, YouTube, and Reddit. Free tier: 500 credits per month.

### Setup steps
- [ ] Install the **Scavio** community node: Settings -> Community Nodes -> install `n8n-nodes-scavio`.
- [ ] Get a free Scavio API key at https://dashboard.scavio.dev (500 credits/mo, 1 credit per Amazon product call).
- [ ] Create a Google Sheet with one tab; add three columns: `product_url`, `price_threshold`, `last_alerted_price` (leave blank). Add one row per product.
- [ ] Attach credentials: Scavio API on **Live price**, Google Sheets OAuth2 on **Read watchlist** + **Save state**, Gmail OAuth2 on **Email**.
- [ ] Open **Read watchlist** and **Save state** and pick your spreadsheet/tab from the dropdown.
- [ ] Open **Email** and set `sendTo` to your alert recipient.
- [ ] Activate the workflow.

### Tips
- To re-arm an alert (you checked but didn't buy), clear the `last_alerted_price` cell.
- To stop tracking, delete the row.
- Drop the schedule from 6h to daily to fit ~16 products on the free tier.

### How can we improve this workflow?
### [>>> Share your feedback at scavio.dev/feedback](https://scavio.dev)

## 1. Read watchlist

Fires every 6 hours and reads every row from the Google Sheet (one row = one product).

## 2. Get current price

For each product, throttles to 2s between requests, extracts the ASIN from any Amazon URL format, and calls Scavio for the live price.

## 3. Decide and notify

Edge-triggered: alerts only when the price drops below threshold for the first time, or drops further than the previously alerted level. Sends a Gmail alert and writes `last_alerted_price` back to the sheet so the next run knows.

parameters.subject: =Price hit target: {{ $json.title ?? $json.productUrl }}
